---
title: "Module 5"
subtitle: "Module 5: HPC, Nextflow and Genpipes"
description: "Practical session"
author: "Alie Rangel (Adapted from Rola Dali)"
date: 2026-05-01
date-format: short
title-block-banner: "#ffffff"
title-block-banner-color: "#2fc1c1"
format:
  html:
    highlight-style: atom-one
    sidebar:
      contents: auto
    code-fold: false
    css: style.css
#    code-block-bg: "#d8e6f4"
#    code-block-border-left: "#0275e1"
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-title: "HPC, Nextflow and Genpipes"
    toc-expand: 4
    toc-depth: 4
    embed-resources: true
    other-links:

      - text: GitHub
        icon: github
        href: https://github.com/QLS-MiCM/HPC_Nextflow_GenPipes
    grid:
      sidebar-width: 300px
      body-width: 1000px
      margin-width: 100px
      gutter-width: 5rem
engine: knitr
execute:
  echo: true

---
```{r,echo=FALSE}
htmltools::img(src = knitr::image_uri("images/MiCM.png"), 
               alt = 'logo', 
               style = 'position:absolute; top:0px; left:30px; padding:10px; max-width:25%;')
```
```{r,echo=FALSE}
htmltools::img(src = knitr::image_uri("images/C3G-Logo-Colour-EN.png"), 
               alt = 'logo', 
               style = 'position:absolute; top:120px; left:30px; padding:10px; max-width:20%;')
```
## Overview
This session will introduce you to how to prepare input files to run Nextlfow and GenPipes RNAseq pipelines.

## Nextflow
Use the online [nf-core tool online](https://oldsite.nf-co.re/launch) to create a configuration file for the rnaseq pipeline version 3.26.0.

Use the following parameters:

- Path to the sample sheet: `/home/User/projects/rrg-PIname/User/RNAseq_Module5/Data`
- Use the singularity profile
- Output directory: `/home/User/scratch/RNAseq_Module5/`
- Use the human genome as reference: GRCh38
- Use fastp trimmer
- Align using bowtie2

<details>
<summary>Click to view example output </summary>
::: {style="max-height: 200px; overflow: auto; border: 1px solid #ccc; padding: 10px; background-color: #f9f6f6;"}

nf-params.json

```text
{
    "input": "\/home\/User\/projects\/rrg-PIname\/User\/RNAseq_Module5\/Readset.csv",
    "outdir": "\/home\/User\/scratch\/RNAseq_Module5\/",
    "genome": "GRCh38",
    "trimmer": "fastp",
    "aligner": "bowtie2_salmon"
}
```
:::
</details>

## Genpipes

### Preparing input files

The input files to run this pipeline are the following:

- All pipelines require 2 files:

  - [Configuration “.ini” file]{.underline style="color: #a80707;"}: contains parameters used by tools in the pipeline

  - [Readset file]{.underline style="color: #f38907;"}: contains details about your samples

- Optional:

  - [Steps selection]{.underline style="color: #06c31c;"}: defines the steps of the pipeline to be run

- A few pipelines require:

  - [Design file]{.underline style="color: #c309d8;"}:  contains details about sample comparisons (e.g. RNA-seq, ChiP-seq, etc…)

![](images/Genpipes_input.png){width=50% fig-align="center" fig-cap=""}

####  Choosing Configuration / .ini files
1. Select the base .ini file for the dnaseq pipeline `$GENPIPES_INIS/rnaseq_light/rnaseq_light.base.ini`
2. Select the .ini file for the server you are using, for example Narval: `$GENPIPES_INIS/common_ini/narval.ini`
3. Select the genome .ini file. Here we will use human genome pipeline: `$MUGQIC_INSTALL_HOME/genomes/species/Mus_musculus.GRCm38/Mus_musculus.GRCm38.ini`

#### Creating the Readset file
The readset file is a tab separated file that describes the sequencing data and to which sample it belongs to:

 - Readset used for analysis
 - Samples to be merged
 - Location of FASTQ/BAM input files.

Create a Readset file in Excel using the samples from the GSE311541 [GEO accession muber](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE311541). These samples come from Mus musculus embryos with and without cardiomyocyte-specific Pkn2 knockout (cKO). Prepare a readset file for all samples.

Start by pasting this headers in a new Excel sheet:
```markdown
Sample  Readset Library RunType Run Lane  Adapter1  Adapter2  QualityOffset BED FASTQ1  FASTQ2
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Look at what library preparation kit they used to choose the adapters. Some of the most popular used kits can be found on this [page](https://support-docs.illumina.com/SHARE/AdapterSequences/Content/AdapterSeq.htm).
::: 

Make sure to save it as a tab-separated file.

<details>
<summary>Click to view answer </summary>

::: {.table-dna }

  | Sample | Readset | Library | RunType | Run | Lane | Adapter1 | Adapter2 | QualityOffset | BED | FASTQ1 | FASTQ2 |
  |--------|---------|---------|---------|-----|------|----------|----------|---------------|-----|--------|--------|
  | GSM9326769_ko | R1 |  | paired_end | SRR36201369 | 1 | AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCA | AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT |  |  | GSM9326769_R1.fastq.gz | GSM9326769_R2.fastq.gz|
  | GSM9326770_ko | R1 |  | paired_end | SRR36201377 | 1 | AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCA | AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT |  |  | GSM9326770_R1.fastq.gz | GSM9326770_R2.fastq.gz|
  | GSM9326771_ko | R1 |  | paired_end | SRR36201376 | 1 | AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCA | AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT |  |  | GSM9326771_R1.fastq.gz | GSM9326771_R2.fastq.gz|
  | GSM9326772_ko | R1 |  | paired_end | SRR36201375 | 1 | AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCA | AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT |  |  | GSM9326772_R1.fastq.gz | GSM9326772_R2.fastq.gz|
  | GSM9326773_ctrl | R1 |  | paired_end | SRR36201378 | 1 | AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCA | AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT |  |  | GSM9326773_R1.fastq.gz | GSM9326773_R2.fastq.gz
  | GSM9326774_ctrl | R1 |  | paired_end | SRR36201374 | 1 | AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCA | AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT |  |  | GSM9326774_R1.fastq.gz | GSM9326774_R2.fastq.gz
  | GSM9326775_ctrl | R1 |  | paired_end | SRR36201373 | 1 | AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCA | AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT |  |  | GSM9326775_R1.fastq.gz | GSM9326775_R2.fastq.gz
  | GSM9326776_ctrl | R1 |  | paired_end | SRR36201372 | 1 | AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCA | AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT |  |  | GSM9326776_R1.fastq.gz | GSM9326776_R2.fastq.gz

:::
</details>

#### Creating the Design file
Many pipelines need a design file to indicate the comparison to do differential expression analysis and statistical modeling.

Use Excel to create a design file that compares the two groups of sample used to build the Readset file (control vs knockout). 

Start by pasting these headers in a new Excel sheet:
```markdown
Sample  Contrast1
```

::: {.callout-note}
The sample names should be written exactly as in the “Sample” column of the Readset file.
:::

Make sure to save it as a tab-separated file.

<details>
<summary>Click to view answer </summary>

::: {.table-dna }

  | Sample | Contrast1 |
  |--------|---------|
  | GSM9326769_ko | 1 |
  | GSM9326770_ko | 1 |
  | GSM9326771_ko | 1 |
  | GSM9326772_ko | 1 |
  | GSM9326773_ctrl | 2 |
  | GSM9326774_ctrl | 2 |
  | GSM9326775_ctrl | 2 |
  | GSM9326776_ctrl | 2 |

:::
</details>

### Creating GenPipes command script
You are ready to run the GenPipes command to generate the pipeline script once you have all the input elements for the pipeline:

- configuration files are identified
- select alla steps in the rnaseq_light pipeline
- readset and design files are ready

The GenPipes command will look like this:
```{bash}
#| eval: false
genpipes rnaseq_light 
	-c $GENPIPES_INIS/rnaseq_light/rnaseq_light.base.ini \
	$GENPIPES_INIS/common_ini/narval.ini \
	$MUGQIC_INSTALL_HOME/genomes/species/Mus_musculus.GRCm38/Mus_musculus.GRCm38.ini \
	-r readset.txt \
	-s 1-8 \
	-d design.txt \
	-g rnaseq_light_genpipes.sh\
	-o /homo/USER/scratch/c3g-workshop-M3/Genpipes_output \
```

You will get the GenPipes script with all the nessesary information and dependence structure to run the pipeline, under the name of `rnaseq_light_genpipes.sh`.

::: {.callout-caution}
The Genpipes command does not run the pipeline. You still need to execute the script that was created.
```{bash}
#| eval: false
bash rnaseq_light_genpipes.sh
```
::: 

### Outputs
As jobs finish, log files will be created in the “job_output” folder.​

Additionally, for every analysis step, a folder will be created with the appropriate output files.

Look at the files in the following link to get familiar to [GenPipes rnaseq pipeline output](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/10VYIhmSlIqk_Pemm5Em_148axZx4Tmt0?usp=sharing). It is a good time to ask questions about the files.

::: {.content-visible}
<div style="margin-bottom: 1000px;"></div>
:::
